rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical three-component thiol-ene/perfluoroalkylation of styrenes with arylthiols and perfluoroalkyl iodides to give β-thio-perfluoroalkylated products via a radical pathway
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction generates the perfluoroalkyl radical (nC4F9•) from nC4F9I; the perfluoroalkyl radical adds to the styrene to form a benzylic C-radical that is trapped by the arylthiol/thiyl species

key_intermediates.name: benzylic C-radical from nC4F9• addition to 4-vinylbiphenyl

electron_flow: Cathode reduces nC4F9I to give nC4F9• which adds to the styrene producing a benzylic radical; this carbon radical is captured by the arylthiol (or its thiyl radical) to deliver the ArS group, completing the three-component coupling.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Product 4aa only trace; TEMPO-benzylic adduct B detected by LC-MS ([M+H]+ 556.4, calc 556.57)

probe_reagents.name: diaryl disulfide 2a' (in place of thiol 2a)
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace 4aa formed, indicating disulfide is not a competent intermediate under the standard conditions

notes: Incomplete suppression by BHT (70%) and DPE (42%) suggests the benzylic radical is short-lived/efficiently trapped before scavenger interception; only TEMPO fully suppresses and gives a detectable adduct. Control with the disulfide 2a' giving only trace product argues against a disulfide-derived thiyl radical pathway under the standard electrochemical conditions.
